paper_title: Lower bounds for graph reconstruction with maximal independent set queries
summary: Problem 5.1 from arXiv:2404.03472 asks whether $(1,r)$-cover-free families achieving $t\in\mathcal{O}(r^2\log|\mathcal{F}|/\log r)$ exist, which would close the longstanding gap $\Omega(r^2\log n/\log r)\leq t(n,1,r)\leq\mathcal{O}(r^2\log n)$ that has been open since the 1990s. A related ITCS 2025 follow-up paper on graph reconstruction via MIS queries addresses the algorithmic bounds from the same source paper but does not resolve this combinatorial problem about cover-free families. No post-2024 paper closing the $(\log r)$-factor gap for $(1,r)$-cover-free families was found in the indexed literature.
search_queries: cover-free families '1,r' optimal bound r^2 log combinatorics 2024 2025
Michel Scott 'graph reconstruction' 'maximal independent set' 'cover-free' arXiv 2404.03472
cover-free families optimal t r^2 log n log r upper bound construction 2024 2025 2026 arXiv combinatorics
notes: The gap between the lower bound Omega(r^2 log n / log r) and upper bound O(r^2 log n) for t(n,1,r) has been known since the 1990s (cf. Electronic Journal of Combinatorics v23i2p45 for a survey of lower bounds). The ITCS 2025 paper 'Graph Reconstruction via MIS Queries' (LIPIcs ITCS 2025, doi:10.4230/LIPIcs.ITCS.2025.66) is a direct algorithmic follow-up to the source paper but does not address Problem 5.1. No construction achieving the conjectured O(r^2 log n / log r) bound was found.
